title: synaphea acutiloba
intro: Synaphea acutiloba, also known as the prickly synaphea, is a shrub native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antibacterial and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Synaphea acutiloba include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Traditional herbal preparations of Synaphea acutiloba include making a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
